workers.name: narrow-crm-broad-grants
workers.task:
Drive the 2 remaining BROAD GRANTs to PASS by replacing the wide `preHandler: requireCrmEditor` and `preHandler: requireCollectionEditor` calls on the routes that target `crm.deal.create` and `crm.quote.send` with the narrow `preHandler: requirePerm('<permKey>')` form using the existing Wave 5 narrow perm sets.

CURRENT LINT STATE (before this work):
  ✓ 37 PASS — catalog grants ⊆ legacy allow-list
  ✗ 2 BROAD GRANT — `crm.deal.create` (extra SalesLead/SalesManager/SalesRep/ServiceManager) and `crm.quote.send` (same extras)
  ? 9 NO LEGACY ALLOW-LIST
  0 UNKNOWN

GOAL: 39 PASS / 0 BROAD / 9 NO LEGACY / 0 UNKNOWN.

APPROACH:
1. `git grep -n "requireCrmEditor\|requireCollectionEditor" server/app.js` to find every route that uses the wide helpers.
2. For each route, check whether the route body or the helper comment says it grants `crm.deal.create` or `crm.quote.send` perm. If yes, replace the wide preHandler with `preHandler: requirePerm('crm.deal.create')` or `preHandler: requirePerm('crm.quote.send')` respectively. requirePerm is already exported from server/rbac/preHandler.js — just import it at the top of app.js if not already there.
3. Make sure the existing `DealCreator` and `QuoteSender` narrow perm sets (defined in server/rbac/matrix.js, granted to the right roles in server/rbac/roleMatrix.js) cover all the legacy roles the broad helpers allowed. Look at the matrix.js descriptions — they say who holds each narrow set. If a role is missing, add it to roleMatrix.js for that narrow perm set, NOT to the broad helper.
4. Add a comment above each converted route: `// rbac-audit: expected-roles Owner, Admin, Accountant, SalesLead, SalesManager, SalesRep, ServiceManager` so the linter can re-derive the allow-list. (This is the same pattern Wave 5 and Wave 7 used.)

CONSTRAINTS:
- DO NOT touch routes that target other perm keys (the broad `requireCrmEditor` may also be used for `crm.contact.*` and `crm.activity.*` perms — those perms are not in the 2 remaining BROADs, so leave them on the wide helper).
- DO NOT change matrix.js or roleMatrix.js unless a role is genuinely missing from a narrow perm set's allow-list.
- DO NOT modify the wide helpers themselves.

VERIFICATION (must pass before committing):
1. `node scripts/lint-rbac-broad-grants.js` — must show 39 PASS / 0 BROAD / 9 NO LEGACY / 0 UNKNOWN
2. `node scripts/snapshot-catalog-grant-audit.js` to regenerate the snapshot fixture
3. `node --test test/api.test.js 2>&1 | tail -5` — must show 233/233 pass
4. `node --test test/rbac-broad-grants.test.js test/rbac-migration.test.js 2>&1 | tail -5` — must show 0 fail

DELIVERABLE: One commit on branch narrow-crm-broad-grants titled `Wave 8: narrow final 2 BROAD GRANTs (crm.deal.create + crm.quote.send)`. Push to origin. Write handoff.md with the lint delta and the route file:line of each conversion.

workers.name: annotate-no-legacy-sites
workers.task:
Annotate the 9 NO LEGACY sites so the linter can compute an allow-list for them. Each annotation is a single comment line inside the helper body or above the route declaration: `// rbac-audit: expected-roles Owner, Admin, <role>, ...`.

CURRENT NO LEGACY SITES (from docs/CATALOG_GRANT_AUDIT.md):
  1. `requireAnalyticsReportReader` → `analytics.report.read`  (helper in server/rbac/ — find the body)
  2. `GET /api/pilots/templates/clinic-wellness` → `pilot.template.read`
  3. `POST /api/pilots/templates/clinic-wellness/install` → `pilot.template.install`
  4. `GET /api/pilots/clinic-wellness/owner-briefs` → `pilot.brief.read`
  5. `POST /api/pilots/clinic-wellness/owner-briefs` → `pilot.brief.create`
  6. `GET /api/pilots/clinic-wellness/operator-workbenches` → `pilot.workbench.read`
  7. `POST /api/pilots/clinic-wellness/operator-workbenches` → `pilot.workbench.create`
  8. `GET /api/pilots/clinic-wellness/accountant-reviews` → `pilot.review.read`
  9. `POST /api/pilots/clinic-wellness/accountant-reviews` → `pilot.review.create`

GOAL: each annotation lists the roles the route is INTENDED to be open to. Read the route body / `req.user` checks to determine intent. The pilot routes appear to be role-keyed (owner briefs → Owner; operator workbenches → Owner/Operator; accountant reviews → Owner/Accountant/Auditor) — confirm by reading the handler.

APPROACH:
1. For each helper: find the body of `requireAnalyticsReportReader` in server/rbac/. Add a `// rbac-audit: expected-roles Owner, Admin, Auditor` line near the top of the function body (the lint script honors annotations inside helper bodies).
2. For each route: find the route declaration in server/app.js. Add a `// rbac-audit: expected-roles ...` line on the line just above the route. The lint script reads these for the inline `preHandler: requirePerm(...)` style.
3. After adding all 9 annotations, the linter will compute an allow-list for each and report it as a new PASS or NO LEGACY. If your annotation is empty (no roles), that's also fine — the linter will mark the route as `_ANNOTATED_BUT_EMPTY_` and not flag it as NO LEGACY.

CONSTRAINTS:
- The annotations are DOCUMENTATION — do not change route behavior. No preHandler changes, no role matrix changes, no perm set changes.
- Touch only server/rbac/guards.js (or wherever requireAnalyticsReportReader lives) and server/app.js.
- Use the same comma-separated role list format as the existing `// rbac-audit:` comments in server/app.js (e.g. `Owner, Admin, Operator, Accountant`).

VERIFICATION:
1. `node scripts/lint-rbac-broad-grants.js` — must show NO LEGACY count drop (preferably to 0 if all 9 annotations are non-empty, or to a smaller number if some are empty).
2. `node scripts/snapshot-catalog-grant-audit.js`
3. `node --test test/api.test.js 2>&1 | tail -5` — 233/233 still pass (no behavior change)
4. `node --test test/rbac-broad-grants.test.js 2>&1 | tail -5` — 0 fail

DELIVERABLE: One commit on branch annotate-no-legacy-sites titled `Wave 8: annotate 9 NO LEGACY sites for linter visibility`. Push. Write handoff.md listing each annotation added (file:line + roles).

workers.name: add-inventory-adjust-perms
workers.task:
Finish the Wave 7 Worker B work that hit silent-success failure. Register the missing inventory + stock adjustment permission keys in server/rbac/permissions.js that the matrix.js InventoryOperator/InventoryAdmin perm sets reference but the catalog does not yet know about.

PERM KEYS TO REGISTER (check which are missing first):
  - inv.stock.adjust
  - inv.stock.deliver
  - inv.stock.transfer
  - inv.stock.scrap
  - inv.stock.count
  - inv.product.delete
  - inv.valuation.run

APPROACH:
1. Read server/rbac/permissions.js. Look for the inventory + stock categories. List which of the 7 keys above are already present.
2. For each missing key, add an entry in the form: `{ key: 'inv.stock.adjust', category: 'inventory', description: 'Adjust stock levels (count, recount, reconciliation)' }`. Use the existing entries as a style template.
3. Do NOT add new role grants — these keys are already held by roles that have InventoryOperator / InventoryAdmin perm sets, but the catalog was incomplete so the linter reported them as `unknown-key`. Adding the keys drops UNKNOWN to 0.
4. Do NOT touch matrix.js or roleMatrix.js.

VERIFICATION:
1. `node scripts/lint-rbac-broad-grants.js` — UNKNOWN count must drop to 0 (or stay at 0 if already 0).
2. `node --test test/api.test.js 2>&1 | tail -5` — 233/233 still pass.
3. `node --test test/rbac-broad-grants.test.js 2>&1 | tail -5` — 0 fail.

DELIVERABLE: One commit on branch add-inventory-adjust-perms titled `Wave 8: register missing inventory adjustment perm keys`. Push. Write handoff.md listing the keys added (or noting that all were already present).
